questions.q: What is the general formula for aldehydes?
questions.explanation: Aldehydes have a carbonyl group (C=O) at the end of the carbon chain, hence the general formula is R-CO-H.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of an aldehyde?
questions.explanation: Formaldehyde is an aldehyde because it has a carbonyl group at the end of the carbon chain.

questions.q: What is the name of the reaction where an aldehyde reduces to an alcohol in the presence of hydrogen gas?
questions.explanation: The reduction of aldehydes to alcohols in the presence of hydrogen gas is a common reaction in organic chemistry.

questions.q: Which of the following carbonyl compounds is a ketone?
questions.explanation: Acetone is a ketone because it has a carbonyl group in the middle of the carbon chain.

questions.q: What is the product of the reaction between acetone and sodium bisulfite?
questions.explanation: The reaction between acetone and sodium bisulfite produces acetone bisulfite.

questions.q: What is the difference between a primary and a secondary alcoholic group in a carboxylic acid?
questions.explanation: A primary alcoholic group has one carbon attached to the carbon with the hydroxyl group, while a secondary group has two carbons attached.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a carboxylic acid?
questions.explanation: Acetic acid is a carboxylic acid because it has a carboxyl group (COOH).

questions.q: What is the name of the reaction where a carboxylic acid reacts with a base to produce a salt and water?
questions.explanation: The reaction between a carboxylic acid and a base is called neutralization.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a carboxylate ion?
questions.explanation: Sodium acetate is a carboxylate ion because it is the salt of a carboxylic acid.

questions.q: What is the product of the reaction between acetic acid and calcium carbonate?
questions.explanation: The reaction between acetic acid and calcium carbonate produces calcium acetate.

questions.q: What is the effect of adding a strong acid to a solution of a carboxylic acid?
questions.explanation: The addition of a strong acid to a solution of a carboxylic acid will decrease the pH.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of carboxylic acids?
questions.explanation: Carboxylic acids are weak acids because they do not completely dissociate in water.

questions.q: What is the name of the reaction where a carboxylic acid reacts with an alcohol to produce an ester?
questions.explanation: The reaction between a carboxylic acid and an alcohol is called esterification.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of esters?
questions.explanation: Esters are neutral compounds because they do not have any acidic or basic properties.

questions.q: What is the product of the reaction between ethanoic acid and ethanol?
questions.explanation: The reaction between ethanoic acid and ethanol produces ethyl ethanoate.

questions.q: What is the effect of adding a strong acid to a solution of an ester?
questions.explanation: The addition of a strong acid to a solution of an ester will decrease the pH.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a carboxylic acid derivative?
questions.explanation: Sodium acetate is a carboxylic acid derivative because it is the salt of a carboxylic acid.

questions.q: What is the name of the reaction where a carboxylic acid derivative reacts with a base to produce a salt and water?
questions.explanation: The reaction between a carboxylic acid derivative and a base is called neutralization.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of carboxylic acid derivatives?
questions.explanation: Carboxylic acid derivatives are weak acids because they do not completely dissociate in water.
